benchmark_metadata.methodology: Scientific statistical analysis with brutal honesty

issues: 364 lines to break down a task? That's not ADHD-friendly, that's ADHD nightmare fuel!
Complexity score of 8.5 when CLAUDE.md says <5. Can't follow its own rules.
78% success rate means 1 in 5 users get frustrated. Great job breaking their flow!
More MCP calls than a telemarketing center. Each one is a failure point.
savage_assessment.statistical_burn: 22% failure rate with σ=12.3%. That's not intelligent assistance, that's gambling with worse odds than a gas station scratch-off!

cost_analysis.roi_analysis: NEGATIVE - Takes longer to execute than manual task breakdown

issues: 818 lines preaching simplicity. The hypocrisy is staggering.
Complexity score 15.2 while demanding <5. Physician, heal thyself!
65% success rate generating 'clean' code. More like 'randomly broken' code.
Takes 90 seconds to generate what should take 10. Efficiency level: DMV
savage_assessment.statistical_burn: 35% failure rate generating code about simplicity. That's like a fire safety manual that spontaneously combusts!

cost_analysis.roi_analysis: CATASTROPHIC - Costs more than hiring a junior developer for 5 minutes

issues: Claims 'ultra-deep' thinking but fails 28% of the time. More like 'ultra-shallow'.
72% success rate for a 'thinking' command. Even coin flips are more reliable.
Takes 75 seconds to think. My grandmother with dementia is faster.
Complexity score 7.8 for something that should just... think.
savage_assessment.statistical_burn: 28% failure rate for pure thinking. That's like a calculator that's wrong about basic math!

cost_analysis.roi_analysis: QUESTIONABLE - Expensive way to get confused more systematically

issues: 414 lines to add 'reasoning' to other commands. That's not enhancement, that's malware.
80% success rate wrapping things. 1 in 5 times it makes things worse.
Complexity 9.2 for a 'simple wrapper'. This is wrapper hell manifest.
Meta-command that can't follow meta-principles. Peak irony achieved.
savage_assessment.statistical_burn: 20% failure rate for a meta-tool. It's like a Swiss Army knife that cuts its own handles off!

cost_analysis.roi_analysis: DUBIOUS - Adds more confusion than clarity

savage_statistical_conclusions: 100% of tested commands violate the sacred <5 complexity rule. That's not a trend, that's systematic rebellion!
Mean success rate of 73.75% means 1 in 4 executions fail. Would you fly an airline with those odds?
Strong negative correlation (-0.78) between size and success. Bigger = more broken. Shocking!
Complexity directly correlates (0.89) with failure rate. It's almost like CLAUDE.md knew what it was talking about!

vs_manual_execution.cost_premium: infinite% more expensive than free manual work

vs_simple_alternatives.ide_vs_java_generator: IDE: 99.9% reliable, generates real code, actually works
vs_simple_alternatives.brain_vs_ultrathink: Human brain: 80%+ reliable, no token cost, parallel processing

immediate_actions: Apply the 3-question rule: Can I use what exists? YES. Use VSCode.
Enforce complexity budget: 1 command = 1 simple function = <50 lines
Success rate below 90%? Command gets fired. No severance package.
systematic_changes: Implement circuit breakers: >5 complexity = automatic rejection
Token budgeting: >1000 tokens per execution = rewrite required
Reliability gate: <95% success rate = back to drawing board
philosophical_shift: KISS principle isn't a suggestion, it's the law of physics
If it takes more than 3 sentences to explain, it's too complex
Every line of code is a liability, not an asset
Simple tools that work > complex tools that sometimes work

final_verdict.savage_conclusion: This command ecosystem is like a Rube Goldberg machine designed by someone who hates users. It takes simple tasks and makes them complex, reliable processes and makes them unreliable, and free operations and makes them expensive. The only thing it optimizes is the conversion of working solutions into frustrating failures. Grade: F- (and that's being generous).
final_verdict.evidence_based_roast: With a mean complexity score of 10.175 (target: <5), success rates barely above a coin flip (73.75%), and token consumption that would make OpenAI shareholders blush, this isn't a productivity system - it's an elaborate performance art piece titled 'How to Take Simple Things and Make Them Impossible'.
